title: IT Sales
overview: We are looking for a confident and friendly salesperson to help us find new clients and grow our business across East Africa. You will talk to companies about the IT products and services we offer, build good relationships with them, and help close deals. No deep technical knowledge required — just great people skills and a passion for hitting targets.
responsibilities: Find new clients by reaching out to businesses, attending events, and following up on leads.
Meet with potential clients to understand what they need and explain how our services can help them.
Prepare simple quotes and proposals and send them to clients.
Follow up with clients regularly to keep the relationship going and close deals.
Work with our technical team to make sure clients get the right solution.
Keep track of your sales activities and report progress to your manager.
Attend industry events and networking meetups to represent the company.
Meet monthly and quarterly sales targets.
requirements: A diploma or degree in Business, Marketing, IT, or any related field.
At least 3 years of experience in sales, preferably in IT or technology.
Good communication and presentation skills — you should be comfortable talking to managers and company directors.
A positive attitude, self-motivated, and able to work with minimal supervision.
Basic understanding of IT products like computers, networking, and software.
Willingness to travel within Nairobi and occasionally upcountry for client visits.
Experience using a CRM tool like Salesforce or any similar system is a plus.

title: IT Technician
overview: We are looking for a hands-on IT Technician to set up, maintain, and fix IT equipment and networks for our clients. You will be the person clients call when something is not working, and you will make sure their systems are always up and running. This is a practical, on-the-ground role perfect for someone who enjoys solving problems and working with technology every day.
responsibilities: Set up and configure computers, printers, routers, switches, and other IT equipment at client sites.
Fix hardware and software issues reported by clients quickly and professionally.
Install and maintain network connections including Wi-Fi and cabled networks.
Perform routine checks and maintenance to prevent problems before they happen.
Help clients with basic IT training and show them how to use their equipment.
Keep clear records of all work done, equipment installed, and issues resolved.
Escalate complex issues to senior engineers when needed.
Respond to support calls and visit client sites when required.
requirements: A diploma or degree in IT, Computer Science, Telecommunications, or a related field.
At least 2 years of hands-on experience in IT support or network installation.
Ability to set up and troubleshoot routers, switches, computers, and common software.
Basic knowledge of networking — understanding of IP addresses, Wi-Fi, and cabling.
Good communication skills and a patient, helpful attitude when dealing with clients.
Able to work independently and manage your own time at client sites.
A valid driving licence is an added advantage.
Certifications like CompTIA A+, CCNA, or N+ are a plus but not required.

title: IT Project Lead
overview: We are looking for an experienced IT Project Lead to manage the delivery of large IT projects for our clients, including government offices and big companies. You will be in charge of making sure projects are completed on time, within budget, and to the client's satisfaction. This role is ideal for someone with strong leadership skills who can coordinate teams and communicate clearly with clients at all levels.
responsibilities: Plan and manage IT projects from start to finish, making sure everything runs smoothly.
Meet with clients regularly to give updates, understand their needs, and solve any issues.
Coordinate the internal team — technicians, engineers, and other staff — to get work done on time.
Create project plans, timelines, and progress reports for clients and management.
Identify risks early and put plans in place to avoid delays or problems.
Make sure all work meets quality standards and client expectations.
Train client staff on new systems once a project is complete.
Support the sales team by helping write proposals and attend client presentations.
requirements: A degree in IT, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. A Master's degree is a plus.
At least 7 years of experience in IT, with at least 3 years managing projects or leading a team.
Experience delivering IT projects for government or large companies in Kenya or East Africa.
Good understanding of IT systems — networking, servers, software, and security.
Strong leadership and communication skills — you should be comfortable presenting to senior management.
Experience with project management tools like MS Project or Jira.
A project management certification like PMP or PRINCE2 is preferred.
Willingness to travel within Kenya and the region for client visits and site work.
